windows.name: Single Glazing
windows.notes: Old single-glazed windows with poor perimeter seals; significant infiltration source.

windows.name: Double Glazing (Old)
windows.notes: Pre-1995 double glazing; deteriorated seals give moderate infiltration rates.

windows.name: Double Glazing (Modern)
windows.notes: Modern double glazing with good perimeter seals; low background infiltration.

windows.name: Triple Glazing
windows.notes: Triple glazed units with precision hardware and multi-point locking; very low infiltration.

windows.name: PVC Frame Double Glazing
windows.notes: PVC frames with modern sealing gaskets; typically low infiltration when in good condition.

windows.name: Wood Frame Double Glazing
windows.notes: Timber frames can swell/shrink seasonally; slightly higher infiltration than PVC equivalent.

windows.name: Aluminium Frame (Thermal Break)
windows.notes: Aluminium frames with thermal break and quality gaskets; low air infiltration.

windows.name: Double Glazing with Trickle Vent
windows.notes: Modern double glazing fitted with a trickle vent (background ventilator) above the frame. Provides controlled background ventilation per UK Building Regulations Part F. The vent contributes a guaranteed minimum fresh-air flow on top of normal frame infiltration.

doors.name: PVC Insulated Door
doors.notes: Modern PVC door with foam insulated core and perimeter compression seals; low infiltration.

doors.name: Solid Wood Door
doors.notes: Traditional solid timber door; natural movement of wood and simple threshold seal leads to higher infiltration.

doors.name: Insulated Timber Door
doors.notes: Insulated timber door with modern multi-point seals; improved airtightness over solid wood.

doors.name: Aluminium Door (Thermal Break)
doors.notes: Aluminium door with thermal break and quality compression seals; low infiltration.

doors.name: Insulated Steel Door
doors.notes: Steel door with insulated core and multi-point locking; very low infiltration.

doors.name: Poorly Fitted / Draughty Door
doors.notes: Old or poorly installed door with significant perimeter gaps and no threshold seal. High draught infiltration rate; a draught excluder and door seal kit can reduce this to ~2 m³/(h·m²).
